attacking_style: Not an attacker; operates as a lead-out specialist and positioning anchor in sprint finishes, delivering his sprinter into optimal position in the final kilometers.
team_tactics: Functions as the early-to-mid lead-out anchor for Quick-Step sprint trains, establishing position before final pilot takes over. Moves to front early to control positioning.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint stages where technical finishes reward precise positioning. Excels in challenging conditions (wet, technical turns) where positioning discipline matters most.
vulnerability: Effectiveness depends on team plan execution; when rivals disrupt timing or make surprise moves, his early positioning work can be neutralized regardless of quality.
key_patterns: Serves as positioning anchor for Quick-Step lead-out, moving to front early in sprint stages.
Maintains composure and execution quality in difficult conditions when rival trains falter.
Tactical value diminished when opponents break expected timing patterns or make surprise accelerations.
form_trajectory: neutral: Successfully executing lead-out duties but team outcomes mixed due to external tactical variables
